function.name: read_file
function.description: Read the contents of a file in the workspace. Only files in the workspace directory can be read.

path.description: Relative path to the file within the workspace (e.g. 'AGENTS.md').

function.name: edit_file
function.description: Overwrite the contents of an existing file in the workspace. You cannot create new files or delete files — only edit files that already exist.

path.description: Relative path to the file within the workspace.

content.description: The new complete content to write to the file.

function.name: list_files
function.description: List all files currently in the workspace directory.

function.name: send_file
function.description: Send a file from the workspace as a Discord attachment. The file will be sent after the agent's response.

path.description: Relative path to the file within the workspace.

function.name: edit_config
function.description: Update a single key in config.toml at the project root. This is restricted and requires user approval.

key.description: Config key to update. Use dot notation for sections (e.g. 'provider.ollama.base_url').

function.name: restart_gateway
function.description: Restart the opoclaw gateway. This is restricted and requires user approval.

function.name: hibernate_gateway
function.description: Hibernate the opoclaw gateway (stop responses until approved to wake). This is restricted and requires user approval.

function.name: update_opoclaw
function.description: Update opoclaw to the latest version. This is restricted and requires user approval.

function.name: search
function.description: Search the web and return top results.

count.description: Max results to return (1-10). Defaults to 5.

function.name: use_skill
function.description: Load a skill by name from workspace/skills/<skill>/SKILL.md. Use this before applying a skill's instructions.

function.name: deep_research
function.description: Enable Deep Research mode to perform multi-step research and return synthesized markdown documents.

function.name: web_fetch
function.description: Fetch a web page and return its text content.

function.name: react_message
function.description: React to a Discord message by ID in a given channel.

channel_id.description: Discord channel ID containing the message.

message_id.description: Discord message ID to react to.

emoji.description: Emoji to react with (unicode or custom emoji like name:id).

function.name: request_permission
function.description: Request authorization from the configured authorized_user_id with a custom message. Discord-only.

message.description: Message describing what approval is needed.

title.description: Optional title for the approval prompt.

function.name: question
function.description: Ask a multiple-choice question in Discord and return the selected option.

function.name: poll
function.description: Create a live poll in Discord with dynamic results.

title.description: Optional title for the poll embed.

function.name: shell
function.description: Run a shell command. This is in a sandboxed environment with a bash-like shell. `~` is your workspace, and is the default working directory. You've got all the commands you'd expect, like `grep`, `cat`, `sed`, and so on. However, you don't have access to Python or other runtimes. Treat this as a way to interact with the workspace and files. You can use `grep -ri 'some text'` to search for text recursively from the working directory.

description.description: User-facing description of what you're doing. Like: "Searching through memory files", "Writing to MEMORY.md", and so on. Don't add an elipsis at the end. Keep this concise.
